slide_1_Cover: AI Drift Control for Polymer Manufacturing
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: Materials-science-first inference detects drift before it happens — fundamentally different from statistical post-hoc analysis with structurally superior early warning
Low-data model works with minimal history and deploys non-invasively on top of existing DCS/MES as a complementary layer with near-zero adoption risk and integration cost
Applicable across injection molding, extrusion, and compounding with minimal marginal cost per line — optimized for multi-site and multi-region deployment at global scale
Geometry-based inference engine reconstructs unobservable latent material states from process conditions in real-time, detecting drift before defects actually occur
Inference results integrate into existing control systems for dynamic parameter adjustment — closed-loop augmentation deploys without any factory downtime required
Operator control window delivers inferred material state, drift signals, and confidence scores via real-time monitoring interface for immediate production decisions
Target polymer plants see 30% defect rates with 10-30% rework costs per batch, losing ~8% of revenue and $360K+ annually per production line in hidden waste
Legacy DCS/MES/SPC systems only detect anomalies after defects occur — process drift is identified reactively, making real-time prevention structurally impossible
On-site engineers adjust manually using experience; data analysis takes days, so real-time optimization is blocked and expertise is lost when senior engineers leave
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: Korea has highest manufacturing density with active govt smart factory policy and funding — CEO's polymer supply chain network enables initial reference site acquisition
Chemical and process industry digital transformation now reaching production floor — sensor and PLC data infrastructure mature for AI overlay deployment in 2024-2025
Injection, extrusion, and compounding processes are identical worldwide — polymer material models port across regions with minimal re-calibration and zero new development
Global polymer process optimization budget pool (McKinsey validated)
Korea-China ML process optimization SW market pool
Capturing software budgets in defined industrial clusters — layered on top of MES and QC platforms
Team $12B is global polymer optimization budget pool; validated SW category (line-level AI) is ~$1.0B+ in 2026
McKinsey/Bain confirm yield+throughput improvement in chemicals; $12B is upper-bound budget, not revenue TAM
Korea-China are top polymer hubs with govt smart factory drive; highest demand for AI overlay on DCS/MES infra
INEEJI $9.7M and onepredict $25M Series C validate Korean industrial AI investment — category is fundable
Pilot deployments prove ROI, then expand within the same facility and across the supply chain — the standard adoption motion for industrial software
$60M reflects the pace at which tightly networked manufacturing ecosystems scale new software — driven by rising ACV as deployments deepen

Process optimization AI with $9.7M cumulative funding; closest Korean competitor with direct manufacturing AI overlap
Only polymer-specific latent state inference engine — incumbents offer broad platforms while Nacre detects drift before defects using materials-science geometry models
Non-invasive deployment on existing DCS/MES with zero factory downtime; observe-advise-control 3-stage upsell reduces adoption barrier for risk-averse manufacturers
AspenTech ($16.7B) and AVEVA ($10.8B) strategic acquisitions prove process SW has massive exit value; Nacre targets same value stack as polymer application layer

Progressive ACV expansion per line added; premium pricing on closed-loop mode
Value anchor: ~14% capture of $360K annual loss per line from defect reduction
Site-based B2B SaaS license with per-production-line billing; 3-stage upsell drives step-function revenue expansion
4 active PoCs with 2 WTP signals — 50 outbound to 7 responses to 4 pilots conversion funnel in 4 weeks
Stage 1: Non-invasive Shadow mode PoCs — 50 outbound yielded 4 active technical pilots with 85% meeting conversion rate in post-trackout outreach sprint
Stage 2: Human-in-the-loop advisory mode delivering drift alerts to operators — upgrade path from observation to real-time recommendations per production line
Stage 3: Closed-loop auto parameter adjustment integrated into control systems — full automation upsell drives step-function ACV expansion per customer site
Asset-light SaaS with core modeling and data infra built once — marginal deployment cost near zero for multi-site expansion across polymer production facilities
Process data from shadow and advisory modes compounds model accuracy over time, building a deepening data moat against rivals with each production line deployed
Material models portable across injection, extrusion, and compounding — category expansion requires minimal new development effort per polymer process vertical

slide_10_Investor_Assessment: Polymer manufacturing and adjacent market size are large and will get larger over time. The team's estimates are conservative
Competition exists in adjacent areas but almost no competition in real-time corrective solution market especially in material-level process due to technical difficulties
The team's scientific and technological capacity can create moats and uncopyable references if real-world adoption starts to occur
It's good that the team as secured a few major manufacturers in China and Korea for PoCs while the solution is not an easily explainable line product
Scalable to all polymer-focused precision manufacturers across the world
Strong binding between the co-founders. Highly capable of science and tech as well as hustling attitude from both

Overall Summary : Clear customer and pain definition. Pain is significant enough - already getting attention and implementation support from enterprise customers for PoC. Team equipped with top-notch scientific capacity and professional experience in the domain. Smart and excellent attitude.
Investment Decision Rationale : If they come to the IC with paid PoC agreement, then invest. If not, watch for 4 weeks until late IC

Key Risks : A risk that only a fraction of manufacturers might understand the true value of the solution while the rest will stay reluctant to adopt it considering it a over-optimization. A risk that manufacturers won't allow the team's solution to be collecting data from their manufacturing processes for confidentiality reason
